raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I paid XXXX XXXX XXXX {$780.00} using my Citi credit card ( {$750.00} rebuild + {$30.00} credit card surcharge ) to rebuild a XXXX diesel injection pump. After reinstalling the pump, the engine ran poorly and was difficult to start. My co-owner, XXXX XXXX, made multiple attempts to contact the merchant for correction, but the merchant became unresponsive after XX/XX/year>. 

I submitted a dispute to Citi and provided detailed evidence, including the original invoice, text logs, call logs, and a diagnostic report from XXXX XXXX showing the pump was rebuilt incorrectly ( pressure regulator assembled wrong, advance mechanism improperly shimmed, no pressure, etc. ). 

Citi mishandled the dispute by placing it in the wrong category. Due to unclear wording on Citis web form, I selected No Attempt for whether I requested a refund, because the merchant was already unresponsive. Citi interpreted this as if I made no attempt to contact the merchant at all, which is incorrect. 

Because of this misclassification, Citi requested irrelevant cancellation documentation, even though my dispute involved defective service, not a refund request or cancellation. Citi then denied the dispute with a generic statement claiming no billing error identified, without addressing any of the evidence I provided. 

The merchants work was defective, I attempted to resolve it, and I supplied multiple forms of evidence. Citi failed to review the evidence, placed the dispute in the wrong category, and denied it improperly. My Citi card of 20 years was also closed during this process, which prevented meaningful escalation.
